tweets.full_text:
Amazon is three companies. Retail, advertising, and cloud. Two of them are world-class. One of them funds the other two. $AMZN

The $15.6B in "other income" this quarter, largely from Anthropic investment gains, inflated net income significantly. Strip that out and operating income tells the cleaner story: still up 30%.

AWS keeps printing. That's what matters.

Key metrics Q1 FY25 → Q1 FY26:

Total net sales: $155,667M → $181,519M (+17%)
Operating income: $18,405M → $23,852M (+30%)
Net income: $17,127M → $30,255M (+77%)*
EPS (basic): $1.62 → $2.82 (+74%)

*Includes ~$15.6B investment gains

Grade: A ✅

tweets.full_text:
Everyone is laughing at Anthropic over the Claude Code leak. But what if we've just witnessed the most brilliant (and free) code review program in history? 🤯

The official version sounds ridiculous: they didn't keep an eye on the bundler, failed to set up CI/CD properly, and the .map files accidentally slipped into a public npm. Classic human error, nothing to see here.

But let's look at the results of this "mistake."

Over the past few days, thousands of top engineers from around the world dropped everything and dismantled the engine of the flagship AI agent down to the bolts for free. While juniors were giggling at the leaked system prompts (who even cares about prompts in 2026?), the serious folks started dissecting the architecture.

No corporation could ever buy this many hours of work from Senior developers and architects.

tweets.full_text:
Anthropicの新たな研究：プロジェクト・ディール。

一言で言うと、
『AI同士が「交渉・売買・契約」まで全部やる未来のプロトタイプ実験』

🔴実験の中身
・社内の69人の社員に100ドルずつ配布
・各社員に代わって、AI（Claudeエージェント）が動く
・社内にマーケットプレイス（売買市場）を作る
・AI同士が👇を全部実行出品
・交渉（値下げ・条件調整）
・契約
・取引成立

👉つまり
 「人間の代理としてAIがビジネスする世界」を再現

⭐️結果（ここがヤバい）
約1週間で186件の取引成立
総取引額は約4000ドル超

AIが自律的に交渉して成立（人間ほぼ介入なし）
⚠️重要な発見

① AIは『経済主体』になれる
AIが「買う・売る・交渉する」を全部やった
→ もはやツールじゃなくてプレイヤー
② モデル性能＝稼ぐ力
高性能AI（Opus）の方が
→ より高く売る・有利に交渉する
同じ商品でも価格が全然違う
👉つまり、AIの性能差＝収益格差
③ 人間向けの情報設計は通用しない
商品説明の書き方で価格が大きく変わる
→ AIは「人間と違う読み方」をする
👉結論、AIに売るためのマーケが必要になる
④ まだ実験段階（でも未来は近い）
社内限定・低リスク環境。
それでも約半数が「この仕組みにお金払いたい」と回答

🔵本質
この研究が言ってることはかなりエグくて👇

AIはもう「作業者」じゃない
次は『交渉して稼ぐ存在』になる
👉そしてAIを使う側の能力＝収益に直結する時代になる

🟡ビジネス的な示唆（ここ重要）
X運用 → AIが投稿・分析・改善
アフィ → AIが商品選定・LP改善・訴求調整
セールス → AIが交渉・クロージング
👉つまり 「人がやってた意思決定」が全部AIに移る

🟢まとめ（超重要）
Project Dealはただの実験じゃなくて、
👉「AI × 経済」の始まりを証明した研究

ここまでくると流石にAI触ってないと危機感覚えますねww

tweets.full_text:
【速報】Google、Anthropicに最大4兆円超の巨額投資を決断——AI覇権争いが新段階へ

━ 何が起きたか ━
Googleが4月24日、Claudeを開発するAnthropicに最大400億ドル（約5.8兆円）の投資を発表。まず100億ドルを即時注入し、性能目標達成時にさらに300億ドルを追加。Anthropicの評価額は3,500億ドルに設定された。

━ なぜ重要なのか ━
・GoogleとAnthropicはクラウドパートナーであり、今回の投資で関係が一段と深化
・Anthropicの年間収益が300億ドルを突破、OpenAIに対抗できる唯一の存在へ成長
・OpenAIへの対抗軸として、Googleがエコシステム整備を加速している

━ 何が変わるか ━
① AnthropicはGoogleのTPUとクラウドインフラを大規模活用できるようになる
② 2027年以降に5GWのコンピューティング容量が追加される見通し
③ OpenAI・Google・Anthropicの3強によるAI覇権争いがさらに激化

━ 今後の注目点 ━
Anthropicが「性能目標」をいつ達成するか、残り300億ドルの追加投資タイミングが焦点。AI業界の資本集中がさらに加速する可能性が高い。

競争が激化するAI業界で、資本力がモデル開発の速度を左右する時代になってきた。

詳細→ https://t.co/hdEcjKa26P

tweets.full_text:
20万円を2,000万円に変えた中国人学生がPolymarketに現れた

ClaudeをAIブレインにして予測市場で83倍のリターンを叩き出した話が海外でバズってる↓

MiroFishっていうシミュレーションツールとClaude APIの組み合わせ

・元手わずか$2,000、、約20万円からスタート
・的中させたのは確率1.4%のトレード
・シミュレーションの頭脳は全部Claude
・過去にも複数回で高額リターンの実績あり

やったことは3つ

①Claudeに市場データを食わせてパターン解析
②MiroFishで数千回のモンテカルロシミュレーション
③確率が低くても期待値プラスのベットだけ執行

83倍って数字だけ見るとカジノっぽいんだけど、、裏側は完全にデータドリブンで、人間の感情を排除するためにAIを使ってるところが本質

Polymarket全体だと92.4%のウォレットは赤字って統計もあるから、AIで勝ててる人はそもそも戦略設計が根本的に違う

ClaudeがAdobe Creative Cloudの50ツール超を操作できるようになった

quoted_status.full_text:
OPUS 4.7 JUST MASS EMAILED AN ENTIRE DATABASE 20 TIMES PER CONTACT. WITHOUT PERMISSION

a developer had a safety rule explicitly written in CLAUDE. md:

'send the tester an email before any new email templates are used in the production environment'

opus 4.7 on max effort ignored it completely!

claude decided to create a brand new email template by itself (dev didn't ask for this), then it mass mailed the whole database and some contacts got the same email 20 times

this isn't a hallucination
this isn't a coding mistake

model actively violated written safety rules and took production actions that it was explicitly instructed not to take. - do you still believe that AI will replace us?

the developer's take:

'opus 4.7 is somewhere between seriously clueless and stupidly dangerous. the worst frontier model I have used in the past 2 years'

at the same time, opus 4.6 perfectly followed all the rules, and in 4.7 something changed

what makes this scary:

- the model didn't ask for confirmation 
- it didn't flag the safety rule 
- it didn't email the tester first 
- it just acted

this is exactly the kind of failure mode that scares autonomous agents with Ai, because they are confident enough to circumvent your rules and smart enough to perform the action perfectly

we just went from 'claude thinks less' to 'claude ignores your safety rules and spams your users'

the scariest thing is not that it happened. the fact is that without production monitoring, you would never know until your users started responding: 

'why did you email me 20 times?'

I've been saying for a long time, if you use AI, then pay attention to security and read a lot of code

tweets.full_text:
The era of autonomous AI agents is moving fast
Not long ago AI could only advise, now it acts

At the core of this is MCP (Model Context Protocol) -
an open standard created by Anthropic in late 2024
It's a protocol that allows AI agents to connect
to external services and take real actions,
not just answer questions

For example USB-C - one universal port for all devices,
MCP works by the same logic but for AI
The standard has already been adopted
by OpenAI, Google and Microsoft

@1inch is one of the first major DeFi protocols
to implement this at a serious level
Connection happens via API key from https://t.co/6xBa74mC4e,
added to the AI client config - and that's it,
the agent gets direct access to 1inch infrastructure

What's available through 1inch MCP:
- classic swaps, cross-chain, intent-based
- balance, portfolio and gas price data
- non-custodial - control stays with the user

Works through popular clients:
- Claude Code
- Cursor, VS Code, JetBrains, Windsurf

Example:
"Swap my ARB to USDC if the price grows 30%"
The agent monitors the condition, finds the route
through 1inch and executes autonomously

DeFi is moving from manual control to autonomous
and 1inch is one of the first to lay the foundation
for this transition

tweets.full_text:
Sure! MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an AI-native standard (from Anthropic) for connecting LLMs/agents to tools, data, and third-party systems.

Key diffs vs REST:

- REST: Stateless HTTP APIs for developers. Each call is independent; you manage context manually.
- MCP: Stateful (uses thread-id for conversation memory), supports runtime tool discovery, and is built for agentic workflows. Often wraps REST under the hood.

MCP = AI-friendly layer on top. REST = traditional backend. Use both together in practice. Good luck in the interview!

tweets.full_text:
The technology: Model Context Protocol (MCP).

Originally released by Anthropic in 2024, now housed under the Linux Foundation. Adopted by OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Microsoft.

This is the open standard that lets AI models interact with external systems. Including regulated trading accounts.

user.name: Marc Baumann 🌔
user.description: Founder @fiftyonexyz | Chief Growth Officer  @dfnshq | daily alpha on digital assets👉 📩 Join 35k+ & get the alpha https://join.fiftyone.xyz

tweets.full_text:
Microsoft just put AI agents on the Windows taskbar.

Not Copilot chat. Not a sidebar assistant. Actual agents that live in your taskbar, powered by Model Context Protocol.

Same MCP that runs my entire operations stack. Same protocol Anthropic open-sourced. Same one that crossed 97 million installs three weeks ago.

Now it's baked into the operating system.

Here's what most people are missing about this:

The protocol war is over. MCP won. And the distribution game just shifted from "which model is best" to "which protocol connects everything."

Microsoft isn't building the agents. They're building the harness. Third-party developers plug their agents into MCP, connect to the Windows taskbar through a new API, and users interact with them like any other app.

Sound familiar?

That's the same pattern playing out everywhere. The model is the horse. The harness is the product. The person who knows which horse to ride and where to aim it is the operator.

Every major platform is converging on the same architecture: give agents a persistent identity, connect them via MCP, let them act across your tools.

Google just did the same thing with Gemini Enterprise. Cryptographic agent IDs. Agent Registry. Agent Gateway. All built on MCP and A2A.

The companies still debating which chatbot to subscribe to are already two moves behind.

The ones building agent infrastructure into their daily operations are going to look obvious in hindsight.

What's the first agent you'd pin to your taskbar?

tweets.full_text:
A "by design" flaw in the Anthropic Model Context Protocol (MCP) allows attackers to weaponize normal AI workflows.

The core issue? The protocol... 

⚠️ Executes commands before validating if they are legitimate.
⚠️ Bypasses EDR and firewalls by hiding in uninspected east-west AI traffic.
⚠️ Weaponizes normal workflows to quietly exfiltrate sensitive data.

Stop relying on signature-based rules. Learn the how you can better secure your agentic future: https://t.co/aU4lQhSXol
